args.title: Q7: how to set issue cardinality (select_one vs open)?

args.text: Re-framing exclusive-vs-independent choices SHOULD be a new issue: the positions mean different things under each reading, so preserving the old issue keeps the record honest. The cost is appropriate, not incidental
args.author: Theorist

args.text: A creation-only flag means a mis-framed issue cannot be fixed without re-raising and re-attaching every position — costly
args.author: Skeptic

args.text: The log records that the fact changed but not a rationale, and the current labelling flips for everyone with no argument attached — the same defect Q4 decided against for decide. Append-only audit is necessary, not sufficient; framing cardinality is a modeling commitment that belongs at creation
args.author: Theorist

args.text: issue_card is already its own fact relation; a move just writes it and the bitemporal log audits the change, so it is not silent
args.author: Proposer

args.text: Changing cardinality after positions exist silently rewrites the attack graph — select_one injects mutual attacks, open removes them — flipping labels with no new argument. That is exactly the silent reinterpretation the immutability ethos rejects (§5.1, Q4 supersession)
args.author: Theorist

args.text: A dedicated 'cardinality <issue> <value>' move that sets or changes it anytime
args.author: Proposer

args.text: A --card select_one|open flag on raise, fixed at issue creation; default select_one
args.author: Proposer

args.text: How should cardinality be set: a --card flag on raise fixed at creation, or a dedicated cardinality move that can change it anytime?
args.author: chazu

args.basis: cardinality is a framing commitment fixed at creation; default select_one; re-framing means a new issue (consistent with immutability ethos, §5.1/Q4)
